feed.title: Lizstar’s Trashcan
feed.subtitle: A place where Lizstar writes about the stupid junk she wants to write about. Special interests include yuri, video games, writing, yuri, obscure video games, and a lot of yuri.

entry.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 214 - Hello Kitty Magical Museum

link.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 214 - Hello Kitty Magical Museum

entry.content:
I have often said, publicly and privately, that the Gameboy Color is one of the worst systems in terms of averages. I can think of like, 4 must play games. Meanwhile, 40% of the library are things like this. Weird cashgrab media tie-ins.

That’s not to downplay the power of Hello Kitty. She’s a multimedia empire for a reason. But even though there have been countless Hello Kitty videogames, and some are actually kinda good, how many people are REALLY chomping at the bit for more? You MIGHT get something good. Or you could get something like this, which is clearly designed to sell more Hello Kitty merch.

This is a very simple game where you have a maze with balls moving around that’ll hurt you and make you reset. You need to pick up all the objects and then reach the exit. That’s it. Dodge the very easy and simple timing puzzles. At the end, you’re rewarded with an image of Hello Kitty.

My favorite one I saw was this. Hello Kitty with a tennis racket with a shirt that PROBABLY is supposed to say “I Love Tennis”, but is NOT centered correctly for her to say “I love Tennis” so it looks very suspiciously like something else.

This game isn’t BAD. But it’s not really much of anything. I’d say it’s best for very young children obsessed with Hello Kitty, and no one else. Or people who collect Sanrio merch.

Also hello, I stopped making these for a few months cause I kind of burned out on Retro Achievements, but I’m slowly getting back to it so it’s time to finish my backlog.

entry.title: LizstarTrek - Enterprise Season 4 episodes 16-21
entry.content:
Every Sunday my Twitch subs and I watch Star Trek in my Discord. Here are my reviews and thoughts on each of the episodes as I see it. If you’re curious about a nerd’s views on Star Trek episodes, please read on, I go quite in depth with some of these episodes! I’ve watched some Star Trek before but not all of it!

IT’S BEEN A LOOOOONG ROOOOOAAAD

Divergence - ENT 3/10

At the end of the last episode, the Klingons put a bomb on the warpcore. Now, we find out that it is set so that it’ll explode if Enterprise goes below warp 5. Holy shit, it’s speed. They need to have the columbia move up next to them, also warp 5, within like 40 meters of each other, and then do some Shenanigans to get rid of the bomb. Specifically, they need to have a tether set up between the two so that Trip can climb up from the Columbia to Enterprise and fix the warpcore.

Well I’m glad they did all that drama getting Trip over to the Columbia top have them just bring him back with this. Totally wasn’t a complete waste of time.

This is how NX class ships fuck.

The climb from Columbia to Enterprise is kind of cool, but it’s thrown out the fucking window when for some reason they don’t just untether the cable and need to make it extra tense for no reason. Also, the plan is to shut down the Reactor, erase some stuff, all while inside the Columbia’s warp field so they don’t go below warp 5. I dunno how to tell you this, but you did NOT need Trip for this. I’m a Trek fan, I can suspend my disbelief for some dumb technobabble shit, but this is Dumb lol. Also it’s a good thing it goes from 0 to warp 5 hot immediately, huh?

Meanwhile, Antaak, the Klingon Doctor, tries to convince Phlox to help him again. Remember, Phlox said “fuck you” and is now sentenced to death. They agree and decide to try and decieve the Klingon General. Cause hey, they’re prolly gonna die anyway, and this way they can save a bunch of Klingon lives? He narrows it down to four possible strains that could cure the virus. One works, three are lethal. It’ll take a week to test safely. They don’t have a week, the fleet will be there in like a day to wipe the colony off the map.

There’s also lots of stuff here with Section 31. It explains why they’re named that way specifically. Article whatever, section 31. He’s like “in times of danger, rule scan be bended :)” “What danger?!” Archer says. “Oh, take your pick.” YEAH, THAT’S KIND OF THE PROBLEM WITH THAT KIND OF RULE.

This is Krell, fleet Admiral. He’s here to blow everything the fuck up. He was actually in cahoots with Harris, the Section 31 guy, but double crossed him when he didn’t need him anymore cause of course he did. He ignores everyone’s pleas that they can cure the virus. Phlox and Archer work to cure the plague while Columbia and Enterprise fight three Klingon cruisers. Phlox needs to directly infect Archer to get some human antibodies or some shit, it’s kind of stupid. They get the cure, and then Plhox sends the virus over to infect Krell intentionally. What the fuck

Do you remember when Phlox’s character trait was being EXTREMELY ethical and never bending on those ethics?

The disease now JUST gets rid of Klingon’s ridges, and does “slight neural realignment”. Dunno how that can be SLIGHT but okay. Also Trip is staying here for Some Reason. JUST to oversee repairs. I’m sure the new Chief Engineer will be happy about that.

Meanwhile, Harris calls up to go “actually them betraying me was the plan :) It all went perfectly, we are not idiots or patsies”. Great. They COULD have had this work out because Section 31 was just idiots, but instead, the Bad Solution Works, like it ALWAYS does with Enterprise.

Also why is Reed still here? Why does he have rank?

Divergence - ENT 2/10

Enterprise is Star Trekkin’ across the universe, and then is found by an Orion, one Harrad-Sar, who at first is aggressive, but then seems eager for business discussions with Archer that could smooth over relations with Starfleet and the Orion Syndicate. No one trusts this with a ten foot pole, especially after last time, where six crew members almost became slaves. But hey, one less enemy COULD be nice.

Meanwhile, Kelby, the new Chief Engineer, is pissed that Trip is still here. Yeah, as expected.

We get a very, VERY drawn out scene where Reed and Archer are horny about some Orion dancers. Yes, green women hot, thank you, get to the point.

Stop trying to convince me Reed is straight. I believe it less than Straight Bashir. To help a mining trade deal go smoother, Harrad-Sar gives them the three Orion slave women. Archer just accepting slaves now, huh? All the men onboard the ship are already being Weird about it.

Yep, it’s pheromones. It’s affecting EVERYONE on ship actually. Even the women, though they’re just irritable and headachy. It’s making Phlox fall asleep. The men are going fucking nuts. Kelby sabatoges the ship because of it. Only two people are not affected……. Trip, and T’Pol. Fuck offfff. It’s cause of their dumb Force Diad thing isn’t it.

They lock the Orions up cause it’s clear they are intentionally sabatoging the ship, though they leave a guard in to watch them. Immediately after Archer was ALMOST convinced to let them free, only stopped by T’Pol. Are these the dumbest fucking astronauts of all time? Why is this crew SO INCOMPETENT?

This episode also fails because everyone is SUPPOSED to be acting out of character but they’re…. not. Archer is angry and belligerent. He ALWAYS IS, ANYWAY.

Is this the MRA episode

Trip and T’Pol manage to stop it and defeat the Orions, and they go off with their tails between their legs. Meanwhile, T’Pol and Trip are annoying heterosexual. Just fucking communicate, y’all.

They kiss, and T’Pol asks him to stay. Trip then admits that he was planning to stay anyway and put in a request to stay 3 days ago. So he WAS TRYING TO TAKE KELBY’S JOB AFTER ALL. BTW they decided Kelby shouldn’t stay chief engineer cause of what he did under the effects of the pheromones. That’s so extremely not fair.

In a Mirror, Darkly - ENT 8/10

We get scenes from First Contact, where Vulcans made… first contact. Remember when Zefram Cochrane tried to salute, then failed, then gave a handshake? Well that almost happens. Then uh, then he pulls out a fucking shotgun and blasts the Vulcans away.

We go to the current day. Things are going well in the Terran Empire. Captain Forrest rules over Enterprise cleanly. His doctor, Phlox, has recently invented a machine for perfect torture. But things are tense with his first officer, Archer. Archer believes he found a future ship in Tholian space, and wants thgem to go after it. It’d be GREAT for his career. So he mutinies, with the help of the sadistic MACO Reed and the other MACOs. He’s now in charge, with T’Pol promoted to first officer, over Reed, who prolly won’t like it. Hoshi was Forrest’s toy, and tries to seduce then kill Archer, but fails.

Archer captures a Tholian. Remember these guys? They’re from TOS and are major antagonists, in the Tholian Web episode, but have never been seen since… but it’s nice to see them again. Archer tortures it for a bit and gets information of where the ship might be, though doesn’t let the others kill it. In fact, Archer has been avoiding killing everyone this episode! Forrest directly says he’s always been docile and has no ambition.

Eventually Forrest is released by T’Pol, who was never on Archer’s side. But Archer had sent a message to the Empire, who wants them to get this ship anyway. Welp. Forrest is back in command, though. We also find she was a spy on board who mindwashed Trip to sabatoge the cloak. Trip is NOT happy about this.

Enterprise gets to where the other ship is, the USS Defiant, a ship from the TOS episode Tholian Web. Cool. Enterprise is caught in a Tholian Web, as Archer and a few others gain control of the Defiant. Forrest sacrifices himself to buy time for the escape pods. Then we watch Enterprise blow the fuck up… to be continued.

I like how overacted this is btw, it’s fun.

In a Mirror, Darkly Part II - ENT 8/10

The Defiant manages to break free of the Tholians and escape, picking up the escape pods. Archer is back in control, though things are tenuous.

The Defiant is a Starfleet vessel from the Prime universe, 100 years later. AKA, a TOS era vessel. It is very, VERY TOS. All the same sound, all the same designs. They even have the same outfits :D It’s very fun. Archer commands they try and fix the ship and give it warp drive so they can go to the front lines and help put down a rebellion. But something is in the crawlways and is killing everyone. They ask one of the slaves they captured… it’s a Gorn named Slar. OH BOY! GORN!

We’ve had Gorn show up like 5 times and EVERY TIME they are drastically different.

What proceeds is them trying to hunt down this Gorn as he sets traps and picks them off. Holy shit. It’s Die Hard. And just like Die Hard, we’re rooting for the Gorn! It’s the second Heroic Gorn!

We get a fight with Archer and the Gorn, and Archer ONLY wins because he cheats by having the grav plating increased to incapacitate the Gorn.

The Defiant arrives to save the last Terran ship from rebel attacks. The admiral on the only ship left is like “yo good job, thanks. I’ll take this ship :) You’ll get a promotion I guess”, so Archer fucking kills him. Oh he’s getting a bit big for his britches. He practically declares that he will become emperor with the power of this ship. Aight dude.

Meanwhile, T’Pol is looking at the ship’s databanks. The idea of a Federation fills her with revolutionary vigor. She’s terrified of the idea of Archer becoming Emperor, he’s a racist fuckhead, so she tries to get Soval, who is now the leader of the dead admiral’s ship, to join her in stopping him and blowing up the Defiant. They also rope Phlox, the only OTHER non-Terran, into joining them.

CAT FIGHT, CAT FIGHT

The plan fails. They stop Phlox, capture T’Pol, and kill Soval. Archer deletes the history, and is like “YAY I’M GONNA BE EMPEROR :D” His sexpot, Hoshi, poisons him, makes out with Mayweather in front of him…

There’s a new woman in charge now, who played everyone and took control.

Demons - ENT 7.5/10

This is what our group considers the “true end” of Enterprise, because of the shit behind the TRUE finale. We’ll get to it though.

We have a weird guy, played by Robocop so I’m calling him Robert Cop from now on, and his lackey making weird comments about a Vulcan baby. Then, we go to a big event where a bunch of different aliens are coming together to form an alliance. The start of the Federation :3 how nice. But it’s kind of ruined by a woman coming in with a hole in her stomach going “THEY’RE GOING TO KILL HER, DON’T LET THEM” and giving a vial of hair over to T’Pol.

Phlox runs the analysis of the hair, and finds out that it’s a Vulcan/Human hybrid of Trip and T’Pol. Trip IMMEDIATELY goes to T’Pol blaming and whining about it. Stop, dude.

Archer gets info that this might be the work of a xenophobic extremist group. “Terra Prime”. To get more information, Archer has Reed contact… Section 31. Ohhh boy. Good. They learn more about Terra Prime, including that the lead dude in charge of the peace conference WAS a member at one point, years ago, and there might be more out there.

Meanwhile, completely out of coincidence, an old flame of Mayweather’s is here to smooch him. She’s a reporter doing stuff on the Peace Talks, but is also IMMEDIATELY under suspect. At least Mayweather is finally getting some, and is getting something to DO. Hoshi last episode, Mayweather this episode. Damn, they did this WAY too late.

Trip and T’Pol go to the moon, as they think that’s where the extremists are, and so is their baby. T’Pol follows the death of another doctor who worked at the same clinic as the girl they found, while Trip tries to get in with the racist assholes. One of their firebrand preachers is this black guy. Man, black men getting behind xenophobic and racist movements. That NEVER goes around to bite them in the ass. Anyways they’re both captured, and we find out Robert Cop, is Paxton, the owner of the mining facility. He’s got a very flat, fascisty affectation. Peter Weller is very good at playing the worst people of all time.

The mining facility is inexplicable a warp capable vessel, and it blasts off to Mars. Meanwhile, Enterprise arrests Mayweather’s girlfriend cause she is, of course, a spy. They chase after, but are too late. Paxton takes control of a super laser designed to stop comets and asteroids and uses it to piss on the moon like he’s Eggman.

See, it’s bad when ALIENS use lasers to kill and terrorize people, but when they do it it’s justified. Because they’re them. Anyways to be continued.

Terra Prime - ENT 7/10

Shit’s Bad. Terra Prime has more supporters than you’d like. I mean yeah, xenophobia sure is a thing on Earth, ain’t it?

The aliens at the conference are UNDERSTANDABLY bothered. Many want to dip. I don’t blame them. But there ARE people there who want to try things to work out, and convince them to stay. They’ll just… blow up the fucking colony and kill thousands of hostages, AND the Terra Prime leaders. And damage future Mars colonization. Oh goodie. Archer has a better option, though. Just, go in and stop them. Easy.

Reed talks to the Section 31 guy and gets more info. They leave on good terms. Oh good, I’m glad that guy is on our side and is a good guy, and I’m not wanting to fucking barf.

So uh, we get a lot of scenes of Robert Cop insulting the Vulcan Human Baby. She’s a clone that he ordered made, and he’s like “IT’S A FREAK OF NATURE THAT SHOULDN’T EXIST”. Why the fuck did you make her then?! What the fuck was your goal? I mean obviously, xenophobes are idiots. One of them goes “you should have thought better before making that freak with the alien and becoming a traitor to humanity”. YOU MADE HER. THEY DID NOT ACTUALLY PROCREATE. YOU DID THAT.

Oh shit, they’re leaving Hoshi as the acting captain on Enterprise. Not that we’ll prolly see her do anything…

lmao Robert Cop has a genetic condition that would get him killed, especially by his fascist savior Colonel Green who he quotes. But he’s still alive despite it being fatal. Cause he’s been taking medicine given freely by Rigelians. He’s a shitty hypocrite. Well, they got xenophobes quite well.

This is the kind of episode they should have written after 9/11, towards all the xenophobic remarks. Not whatever the fuck season 3 was.

Archer’s team makes it in and a firefight ensues. Reed and Trip are shot, and they fail anyway. The laser goes off… but misses anything, hitting the ocean, thanks to Reed sabotaging it. Shit’s fixed.

Things aren’t all good, though. We find out who the ACTUAL spy was, his name was Masaro, and he tells Archer he didn’t actually wanna hurt anyone (then why did you join a terrorist organization) before killing himself. Meanwhile, T’Pol and Trip name their baby Elizabeth, after Trip’s dead sister. But she’s dying due to genetic incompatability.

Archer gives a speach to standing ovation at the peace conference. Things are going to go well. Also, as a twist, Elizabeth died due to cloning problems, NOT genetic compatability :o TIME TO GET FUCKIN’ (also we already knew that, we met their future kid, but don’t worry about it). The episode prolly should have ended on the speach and not this.

There’s also a final episode but we’ve already watched 6 tonight so we’ll watch it later and I’ll append this when we do that next week.

entry.title: LizstarTrek - Enterprise Season 4 episodes 11-15
entry.content:
Every Sunday my Twitch subs and I watch Star Trek in my Discord. Here are my reviews and thoughts on each of the episodes as I see it. If you’re curious about a nerd’s views on Star Trek episodes, please read on, I go quite in depth with some of these episodes! I’ve watched some Star Trek before but not all of it!

IT’S BEEN A LOOOOONG ROOOOOAAAD

Observer Effect - ENT 10/10

Our episode starts with Mayweather and Reed playing Chess and talking very strangely. They are clearly taken over by other lifeforms, contemplating about how they need to be in character for non-interference observation, and they wonder if anyone is going to die today. Well that’s good news.

Travis and Hoshi are returning from a Klingon garbage site, and they get sick. They’re isolated in the decon chamber, first time we’ve seen that for a while. The Observers discuss how when the Klingons and Cardassians faced this illness, they just killed the infected, and expect little to be different. Mayweather’s host is a bit more optimistic, though. He’s also VERY bad at pretending to be Mayweather, being real invasive and asking the infected questions.

We get some Hoshi lore, holy SHIT. She got kicked out Starfleet Acadamy. Why? Because she broke a superior’s arm playing Poker. The superior tried to stop her from gambling, which was against the rules during hours, though she was playing off hours. So she fought back and was discharged. Well, that’s pretty fucking accurate to how that’d be handled.

This episode is actually filled with some amazing scenes with Hoshi and Trip. They’re faced with their possible mortality andu se it as a chance to get to know each other better, and connect. And then occasionally this is interrupted by the observers being really fucking bad at their jobs. The one in Mayweather is kind of a humanist, and is like “we should maybe do more to help these people and keep them alive, I kind of admire them”. They also start taking over other hosts, it’s creepy.

Hoshi starts straight up losing her mind. She hacks her way out of decon and almost opens a fucking airlock. What the hell? She says “I HAVE TO TELL THEM”, so maybe there’s wild shit going on with her, too. Thankfully, Trip talks her out of it, but they end up sedating them to stop them from possibly breaking out. Well, they make Trip sedate Hoshi and himself. He’s like “…I wonder if I’m ever going to wake up again.” This is the most likable and relatable Trip has ever been.

LOL the two observers take over the knocked out Hoshi and Trip, and Phlox sees them standing up like “What the FUCK????” Then he listens in on them talking, and is like “oh okay. Well, time to tell the captain-“ but he’s interrupted by the captain and T’Pol entering in, as hosts. They talk to Phlox about how they’re just here to observe, could help, but won’t. Phlox is disgusted by their behavior. “We are only caught in less than 2% of all cases.” DUDE. YOU ARE TRAGICALLY BAD AT THIS. HOW IS THAT POSSIBLE?

Archer risks his life, taking off his isolation gear, to save Hoshi’s life, under Phlox’s advisory. The Observers are kind of stunned by this? Apparently no one has ever risked their life like this. Turns out Humans are absolutely fucking nuts. Despite everything, Phlox’s treatments fail. Hoshi dies, Trip is likely to die, and now Archer has a walking clock. Also a really good Archer moment tbh.

Archer mourns Trip as he dies… then Trip sits up and scares the shit out of him, which is very funny. They introduce themselves as Organians. Ahh, they were from TOS, cute. Archer wins the nicer one over with his talk of empathy and compassion, and they revive everyone, clear the virus, and then wipe their memories. Then the nicer one goes “I think I’ll make a report about making first contact with the humans. Maybe we’ll be ready in 5000 years or so.”

Over all this was a fantastic episode, ESPECIALLY for Enterprise. Also this is the best Trip has ever been? This is the first episode where he’s fully, ACTUALLY likable and relatable in every way.

Babel One - ENT 8.5/10

The cold open shows Shran’s ship fucking exploding. He sends out a distress call saying they were attacked by Tellarites, and calls for all to abandon ship.

Then we get Archer and Hoshi screaming at each other. Hoshi has a lot of trouble with the food and the dog, and is angy. Archer is angy back. Then, oh, twist, they’re just roleplaying to get ready for the Tellarites to come aboard. Okay, that’s funny. The Tellarites are a founding species of the Federation, but were very VERY rarely seen. Their hat is they like to argue. They’re frank, and kinda rude. I like them. So does Trip. So basically, Enterprise is here to bring the Tellarite ambassadors to a peace talk with the Andorians, across their space. Earth is allowed to travel, Tellarites are not, because of past conflicts. While going through, they hear Shran’s distress call.

Shran is here. He’s happy to see his pinkskin friends, but is NOT happy to hear the Tellarites are onboard. He was bringing his ambassador to the peace talks, but he’s dead now, so whoops! No one is happy about this. Archer is also quite obviously more friendly towards the Andorian’s plight than the Tellarite’s. I kinda hope the episode draws attention to this.

Suddenly, Enterprise is attacked by the Andorians. They ignore Archer, AND Shran. Things seem fishy. The Andorian ship isn’t acting like an Andorian ship, it has things in the wrong place, it’s been upgraded. My theory is it’s a fake attack by the Romulans or something to stoke war and instability. They step away after a weird power failure, and Enterprise is barely spared. Things are very VERY tense between the Andorians, the Tellarites, AND the Humans. The two sides are starting to distrust the Humans, and Archer is worried that they’re moving too fast in trying to mess with diplomatic affairs like this. I mean, if season 3 is any signs, you really should NOT be doing it yourself, Archer.

They catch up to the ship that attacked them and find it looks… different. Turns out… it’s the Romulans! Ey, I actually called it! God, can you fucking imagine if the Romulans were here the entire series??? Enterprise beams people over to look at it, but then the ship comes alive and attacks them. They leave Reed and Trip behind and try and run. Now Trip and Reed are stuck on the ship, which has no oxygen except for one small room, and they begin to explore while their oxygen lowers. The Romulans find out they’re in there, turn off inertial dampeners, and begin to throw them around. I feel like if inertial dampeners were off, they’d turn into paste. But okay.

Meanwhile, Shran’s girlfriend is hot. Sorry, I was distracted. Shran and his girlfriend decide to break out and confront the Tellarite ambassador. She beats the shit out of a guard while in her underwear. Ahhh, why, it’s like Rick Berman is alive and with us today. They attack the Tellarites, but Archer comes in and convinces Shran that actually, they’re being set up by a third party, and he has proof. Then Shran’s girlfriend is shot anyway by a Tellarite. Well that’s gonna make this awfully hard.

Reed and Malcom enter the ship’s bridge, and then we find out the Romulans aren’t even on the ship.

They’re on Myst 3.

To be continued!

United - ENT 9/10

The Romulan spy ship is still flying around, this time exploding a Rigelian ship while disguised as Enterprise. The Romulans are trying really really hard. Reed and Trip are trying to disrupt them from the inside, which is kinda interesting.

Shit’s lookin’ bad. Warrants are out for Archer’s arrest. Shran’s girlfriend is dying, and she wants Shran to kill Tellarites to avenge her, so he’s PROBABLY not willing to help with peace talks. Archer tries anyway. He labels the Romulan ship “The Marauder”, and claims that them being at their throats is what the Marauder wants. Which is true. Shran is actually eventually good with this plan, UNTIL shis girlfriend dies. Then he’s fucking pissed. Kinda understandably, she died for no reason, but tbh that was also his fault.

Reed and Trip have trouble on the Marauder. The Romulans try to kill them. So they fucking blow it up. Whee!

Shran declares ritualistic combat. He refuses to back down, but imagines Archer will wish him luck, cause hey, if he wins, he will do everything he can to get an alliance with the Humans. But Archer isn’t wishing him luck. Because he’s substituting in, and fighting in the Tellarite’s stead. This is VERY TOS. I actually love this moment, because Shran is kind of devestated? He goes “You can’t do this. I’d have to kill you! I don’t WANT to kill you, you’re my friend!” “Then call it off” “…I can’t…” He’s straining against what’s right, what he wants, and what he thinks he has to do for honor and tradition.

Okay, uh, holy shit….

they gay.

Archer and Shran are gay as fuck here. Like, I can’t even begin to go into how gay this shit is, it’s great.

Archer manages to overwhelm Shran, who is like “I WILL NEVER GIVE UP”. Archer goes wrong answer, swings… cut to commercial. Commercial ends, turns out he just cut off his antennae. That’s fucking great. “THEY TAKE 9 MONTHS TO GROW BACK, YOU ASSHOLE.” But when one fighter is rendered helpless, the fight is over. Archer respected Andorian tradition. The matter is settled.

The temporary alliance finds the Marauder, but they can’t blow it up, that’ll kill Reed and Trip. THey rescue them, but the Marauder gets away. This settles the alliance though, they have a common enemy. And on Romulus, the Marauder gets back, they disconnect the pilot… who is a pale Andorian. DUN DUN DUN!

The Aenar - ENT 7/10

They need to go after the Marauder. They use the scans they have to determine that the ship is piloted by a telepath…. but what kind of telepath? It’s Andorian. Kind of… but not quite. The Andorians look at it and acknowledge it’s Aenar. The Aenar are an offshoot of the Andorian people who were once thought of as a myth. Only a thousand exist now, and they are highly telepathic. They are also highly pacifistic, so the idea that one is killing people is confounding. Shran and Archer go under the Andorian ice to try and find them.

You MAY know the Aenar… Strange New Worlds had one! Hemmer, one of my favorite Trek characters of all time.

Meanwhile, T’Pol is putting herself as risk to test their prototype of the telepathic device. Trip is very unhappy about this cause of their romance, and they are all very awkward about this. Well, this triple parter has been extremely good so far, so it had to fuck something up SOMETIME.

The Aenar city is sick as hell. Shran is injured, and they’re saved the the Aenar, who are friendly and willing to help, but not if it means resorting to violence. They acknowledge that one of their kind, Shreb, disappeared a year ago and might have been abducted. Shreb’s sister, an Aenar named Jhamel, is worried about his brother, and agrees to go with them to try and help. The other Aenar try and stop her, but eventually relent when she makes it clear this is what she wants.

They have a plan, but it’s not clear it’ll work. They will hook Jhamel up to the telepathic device and hack the ships. at first it seems like it might not work, the strain might be too much on her, but she pushes through once they get close to the drone and she senses her brother.

Turns out Gareb was lied to in order to get him to do this. He thought he was the last Aenar. He was being used. He sacrifices himself to blow up a drone ship, and then the Romulans kill him, while Jhamel is connected, so she can see and feel it. Damn ):

But that’s the drones destroyed. Archer destroys the other one. Maybe we should have kept that one as evidence, but whatever. Jhamel and Shran leave, good terms between their people.

Then we get more of this stupid fucking Trip plot. He comes up with a bullshit reason and asks to leave Enterprise. Archer can’t force him to stay, so he leaves. No, stop, please.

Affliction - ENT 6/10

We get a strange cold open with a Klingon guy being tortured. Then we get stuff with Enterprise crew at Earth. Trip is transfered to Enterprise’s sister ship, the Columbia, and we’ll get scenes with him on Columbia. Oh boy, he’s NOT leaving the show, they’re just splitting the show’s attention, lovely. And then Phlox is abducted on Earth. Oh ): Well, they all try and figure out what happened to poor Phlox. Hoshi and T’Pol figure out it was some Rigelians. Meanwhile, Reed is contacted by some dude. His suit seems familiar.

Remember Section 31? The Trek writers sure do. I liked them better when they were basically one dude in DS9 and it was obviously the rambling of a madman. Well, they’re back. And guess what? REED WAS A MEMBER. YEP. HE WAS SECTION 31. HOLY SHIT WHEN I THOUGHT HE COULDN’T BE MORE UNLIKABLE.

Phlox is taken in front of some Klingons. He’s told he’s working to help cure a virus. They couldn’t just ASK him as that’s not the Klingon way. This virus is going to wipe out Klingons, so they need his help. Aight then. I’m sure that’s what’s happening.

And then for some reason, Trip and T’Pol start having psychic dream link connections with each other across space. Holy shit. It’s a fucking Force Dyad.

What the shit???

Enterprise is attackd by… someone. They look Human. But they speak Klingon! I wonder what that’s about :o

Yes, if you were unaware before, this is THAT episode.

We find out what’s happening pretty quickly, as Phlox figures it out. They were trying to make Super Klingons using modified Augment Human DNA. This worked at first, but then they all died in agony. One of them was suffering from flu, which mutated, and made the augmentation airborne. Now everyone’s dying, AND their cranial ridges are dissolving. Yep. Remember that “we do not discuss it with outsiders” scene from DS9? This is what they don’t discuss. We’re solving that little mystery/retcon.

I’m of two minds about this. Sure, it’s handled kinda well, and it’s not unintertaining. But it’s kinda more fun if it’s left up to imagination.

Reed is a fucking shit spy. He tries to stop them from figuring out where Phlox is, and they IMMEDIATELY find out about it. The Klingons that boarded are causing trouble, and make the Enterprise shut down. Dun dun dun, to be continued.

This one was a little more limp than the others tonight, but over all, shockingly good night.

entry.title: LizstarTrek - Enterprise Season 4 episodes 1-10
entry.content:
Every Sunday my Twitch subs and I watch Star Trek in my Discord. Here are my reviews and thoughts on each of the episodes as I see it. If you’re curious about a nerd’s views on Star Trek episodes, please read on, I go quite in depth with some of these episodes! I’ve watched some Star Trek before but not all of it!

And hey, I’m back from GDQ vacation! While I was gone, we watched 10 episodes of Enterprise. So let’s get that buffer back up!

IT’S BEEN A LOOOOONG ROOOOOAAAD (literally in this case, I went up to Pittsburgh, east to NYC, north to Boston, then back down to Atlanta)

Storm Front - ENT 6/10

I can’t believe they called this episode that. Anyways, we ended season 3 with the Aquatics randomly placing Enterprise… in the past? I think? And then Suddenly Nazis? This shit is so dumb. Archer is being transported by the most Nazi Lookin’ Nazi I’ve ever seen. How shitty does it have to feel to be typecast as a nazi?

Trip is still trying to communicate with the CLEARLY archaic planes as they shoot at them. Trip continues to be incompetent into season 4, okay. After they realize they’re in the past (I guess maybe the Aquatics are stuck in the past too?), Trip gets pissy about it. Oh good more of that. Though actually he later apologizes for randomly getting pissy, and T’Pol is like “No, you were justified”. No. Don’t justify Trip being awful.

Why ARE dracula aliens working with the nazis anyway? I guess if you wanna destabalize the Earth, this’d be the best way to do it??

How’d they get this modern image of the White House from 2026?

Yeah so obviously, shit isn’t going well on the war front. It’s 1944, and with the aliens’ assistance, the nazis are occupying the east coast. The US Government has basically left, and now there’s just some resistance pockets, run by the mob. Archer escapes the nazis and is kept in Brooklyn with a black woman named Allie.

Meanwhile, Daniels wanders around on the Enterprise, covered in melty skin. Oh he don’t look good. He explains shit is bad and they have to stop it. Yeah dude, I coulda figured that out myself, nazis are on the East Coast. But yes, we need to stop this. How? Here comes help from an unlikely source.

It’s been a long time since we’ve seen Silik! All this temporal cold war shit from the first two seasons was basically just thrown out, but it’s back now. No one trusts him obviously so he’s shot down and crashes to Earth. They assume up on Enterprise it’s his fault, while Archer is trying to find out more about these aliens helping the nazis. They manage to do a sting operation and capture one. Archer refinds his fucking morals and won’t let them torture the alien, what a surprise. The alien says that they’re stuck in the past and the nazis are building them a conduit to send them back to the future. Aight dog, whatever. Archer takes his communicator as he’s gunned down by the mobsters, who are seperated from Archer and Allie. The two of them communicate Enterprise and are teleported up.

Archer talks to Melty Daniels, who explains that the aliens’ leader is Vosk. If they kill Vosk at this point in time, the entirety of the temporal cold war will be erased. Okay, so let’s do that then, I’m good with that. Then Melty Daniels dies, his second time dying on screen. How long do you think it’ll last? Vosk captures Mayweather and Trip, and is like “You WILL tell me what I know” then he looks out over some repurposed tech.

Eh, this episode was okay? Some nazis DID die. The stakes and concept are actually okay, frankly, but the stakes don’t matter, if I don’t care about the fucking characters, and after 3 seasons, I really, really do not.

Storm Front Part 2 - Ent 6.5/10

This episode starts with a 1940s style black and white propaganda film about Hitler arriving in NYC and being “welcomed with open arms” and it made my fucking stomach curl, and then we get the fucking carebears-ass campfire-ass “ITS BEEN A LOOOONG ROOOOAAD” song immediately after it, and it’s like being punched in the fucking sternum.

We get Vosk having an argument with the Head Nazi, getting fed up with the nazi’s… naziism, and is like “You are insignificant to us. You are garbage. We span timelines, and I could stop you from even being born if I willed it.” They DIDN’T put in what I assume came next, which was “but I’m still stuck in the past so please help me build my time machine so I can stop you from being born kthx”.

??? Okay we learn the turning point. Lenin is assassinated by the aliens, and so they never became communist, and so Germany ignored them and focused on everyone else. That is… not how that’d work, in any way? I do not expect any American to really understand the circumstances of the USSR’s formation, but christ, you’d think you’d actually look it up if you’re righting alt history fiction.

Vosk makes a peace offer. He gives back Trip and Mayweather, and is like “if you help us get to the future, we will fix your timeline. You’re being used by Fascists who try and control the timeline. Pay no mind to the literal nazis behind me, your team are the facists. Why are you looking at me that way?” Archer goes back to Enterprise, saying “Eh, I’ll think about it” but obviously won’t. Also whoops Trip is actually Silik, trying to infiltrate. They take care of him quickly.

Silik stole the time machine schematics, but Archer is like “How were you going to send this to the future to your benefactor?” Dog, you don’t need to send it to the future, things do that by themselves. Anyways plans fall through with the nazis, obviously, Archer wasn’t even CONSIDERING considering that offer, so they team up with Silik instead. Silik has no interest in Vosk’s plan working out, so he’s down with blowing them up too. Aight cool. Enemy of my enemy.

They basically frontal assault, using the insurgents as cannonfodder. Silik helps them sneak in by Odoing through the air vents. The special effects are actually way worse than Odo, and that was like a decade earlier.

Vosk shoots the nazis and is like “alright you’ve outlived your usefuleness, bye bye”

Silik is shot by some nazis and dies. He’s like “You were a worthy rival”. Dude, your reason for existing in this show was literally never written, you were NOT a good rival.

We get an actually cool scene with Enterprise dogfighting some nazi planes, then they blow up Vosk as he’s about to enter the time portal. We get a scene with Daniels like “Hey good job” and Archer’s like “Fuck you dude, this plotline SUCKED, just NEVER BOTHER ME AGAIN” and Daniels is like “aight”. Archer, the audience surrogate. Then the timeline fixes itself.

Look, this was literally them trying to fix the bullshit the Temporal Coldwar put them into, and it wasn’t AMAZING but it was FINE for what it was.

Home - ENT 6.5/10

Enterprise returns home. FINALLY. I guess we can ignore the last two episodes, they’ll never be important ever again. The Enterprise crew have a lot of time to rest as everything settles after the Xindi Nonsense. There’s waves of xenophobia after everything, which might reach a head when Phlox takes a vacation to Earth. Meanwhile, T’Pol goes back to Vulcan to visit family, taking Trip with her as her…. friend.

Meanwhile, remember that Vulcan ship they blew up with the zombies on board? Archer is basically accused of murdering everyone onboard. Archer does not take this well and basically stops right at fist fighting the ambassador, who is, as always, the fucking worst. He’s forced to take a vacation to cool his head. He goes with Captain Hernandez, an old love interest who’s getting an assignment now. Damn, girls allowed to be captains?! Wtf are they thinkin’?

THIS is a fantastic picture. Reed, Mayweather, and Phlox are drinking in a bar, when some racist shithead tries to threaten Phlox out of the bar. Reed and Mayweather start a barfight to protect their friend, which is maybe the best thing Reed has ever done. Phlox breaks it up by scaring everyone by puffing up like a pufferfish.

There’s a LOT of family nonsense with T’Pol and her mom. Her mom was forced to retire, and is pissy about T’Pol calling off a marraige with some dude named Koss, who comes calling. He’s very pushy about this, and for some reason marriage law and customs on Vulcan are really shitty so he’s GOING to marry her even if she doesn’t want him to. Ah, this futuristic culture, Humans really are the barbaric ones.

Oh hey, we DO see the Xindi again! Just in a nightmare sequence. Huh. Archer is suffering from extreme PTSD cause of all the literal actual warcrimes he did. He wants to die. You know what’ll fix that though? Getting into a relationship. That ALWAYS FIXES EVERYTHING.

T’Pol decides to marry this guy. Trip makes it about himself. I want to throw him into the sun. I don’t care which one, point in the night sky and throw.

T’Pol gets married, while Trip is there. Why is Koss even THAT into T’Pol? She’s kinda fucking weird. Literally, in this case. Then the episode just ends as Trip is sad that she’s not marrying him. Ugh.

Borderland - ENT 6.5/10

The Klingons find some transport ship and capture two humans who were on it, who proceed to break free and kill every single one of the Humans on board.

And then we get a REAL sight for sore eyes.

GOD, I MISSED YOU BRENT SPINER. This is Arik Soong, and yes, he’s gotta be an ancestor of Noonien Soong. So yeah, Brent Spiner. The seed is strong I guess. Archer comes to talk to him. The killers of those Klingons were augments, and they were embyros who were stolen by Soong 20 years ago. Now, to divert a war and find out what’s going on, they take Soong into the Borderland, where the ship was attacked. Enterprise is being sent out again already. They even use the same shots from the pilot, which is very funny to me.

We learn what Soong’s deal is when he tries to have a pleasant conversation with Phlox, who is NOT having it. Soong is super into genetic engineering, and believes that yes, just because Humans misused it in the past doesn’t mean it should be illegal forever, we can use it to better effect now! Phlox is like “No you wouldn’t. I have eyes.”

We occasionally get scenes with the Augments new Klingon ship. They’re very Animalistic, “the strongest survive and rule” kinda stuff. It’s pretty unpleasant.

Meanwhile, the Orions FINALLY show back up into Star Trek. And how do they show up? By attacking Enterprise, and beaming some people off to sell into the slave trade. Big Show here is in charge of the slave trade. Archer goes in with Soong to try and rescue everyone, though by the time they get there, some are already sold, like T’Pol, who sold for a HIGH price.

Trip: “What kinda people are these?!” Soong: “Prolly a lot like your ancestors, judging by your accent.”

Holy shit. Anyways they buy the cheapest Human on the market and reverse engineer the shock collar so they can turn them off and cause a prison break. Cool. Holy SHIT, T’Pol gets to hit someone! She kicks Big Show right in the dick. The prison break goes off well, though Soong tries to escape in the chaos. Obviously can’t be trusted. Before he’s recaptured, he signals to the Augments. As Enterprise is being attacked by Orions, the Augments show up to “save” them. Maybe this was a bad idea, as Archer is taken captive by Humans with 5x his strength and “double their intelligence”, which is not a thing.

Soong is freed and escapes. This was a dumb plan. But I guess Enterprise is good at Dumb Plans. Soong makes a declaration that he’s going to rescue all their brothers and sisters. Okay then.

Cold Station 12 - ENT 7/10

The crew go to the planet Soong raised the Augments on, and find an Augment who was left behind. They TRY to talk to him, saying they’re peaceful, with their guns pointed at him. Aight y’all. Anyways he tries to attack them and Archer easily overpowers him. So much for 5x stronger? I guess that’s only important when it’s important.

Soong confronts Malik, the Augment leader, about killing his brother, a thing we saw in the previous episode. He thinks they’re like children. Malik lies about why he killed his brother. Children lie :)

Using info from the left behind guy, who is a “mistake” who isn’t super like the others, and info Trip gains, they head towards Cold Station 12, a joint Starfleet and Denobulan station, which holds the remaining Augment embyros. Phlox is worried because his penpal, the same one from that episode a while ago, Dr. Lucas, is in charge of the facility. The Augments EASILY make it on by capturing a Denobulan ship. Dunno why the Denobulans didn’t just scan it but whatever.

Archer is like “Maybe Soong is right”. Dude. Sure, some of his ideas might have some correctness to it, but uh, that doesn’t fucking matter right now. He raised like 16 children into a cult and is starting wars willy nilly with no regard for sapient life because he believes they’re below his “children”. Who gives a fuck what he thinks?

Not to mention the shit he’s doing to these scientists. The leader of the Augments is whispering in his ear the entire time, and convinces him first to beat the shit out of Dr. Lucas, then to torture another scientist using a deadly pathogen in front of him. Soong is NOT handling doiung this well. He tries to stop it before its too late, cause Lucas doesn’t break, but the Augments aren’t really interested in this.

I will say, this main Augment, Malik, is no Khan. He’s just a shitty bratty kid who thinks he’s better than everyone else.

Archer sneaks onboard, alongside Phlox and the “mistake” Augment, but is easily captured. God, everything they’re doing is just NOT working, is it? And Soong is just not able to control Malik at all, who releases pathogens into the station to kill everyone against his father’s wishes. Shit looks bad… can our heroes make it out?! A THREE parter! Dun dun dun!

The Augments - ENT 3.5/10

Archer and the others manage to escape the poisonous space station, though Malik is still sure he’s dead. He says as much to his dad, who is piiiiiissed. Wow, who knew that the Augments who sent Earth into a fascist hellscape would be bad? Soong is slowly learning he’s made a mistake, but is sticking to his guns for now. He thinks he’s in charge. He’s clearly not.

Malik thinks they should hunt for Botany Bay, the ship that Khan was supposed to have been launched on. Soong is like “thats a myth its not real”. Good thing that’ll never come back to bite us.

Once they find out that oh, Archer is NOT dead, Malik is like “okay we need to take care of them permanently. I have a plan though, father. Let’s do a false flag operation and get Starfleet at war with the Klingons :D” and Soong is understandably like “What the FUCK?” Dude, throw this fucker out the airlock, why are you STILL trying to put up with him? If you don’t wanna do that, make it clear he’s bad and put him in jail or some shit, don’t just let him go get allie-

Aaaaand mutinies, what a surprise.

Soong escapes in an escape pod. Klingon ships have escape pods?!

Soong PLEADS with Archer to stop the Augments from causing galactic war

Archer being a genetic determinist, I kind of hate Archer.

Malik murders the lady augment for helping Soong escape. She tries to defend herself but she’s still a Lady so of course she’s easily killed :).

They manage to catch up and stop them from launching their bomb, and disable their ship. Malik takes this VERY poorly and blows up the ship, killing himself and all the embryos. Oh wait no he killed all his brothers and sisters and then teleported off. Oh yay his face is all burned, so that we can rememebr this is related to Khan :)

Soong is locked back up. He’s like “oh you did away with all my work?” and Archer is like “No, we put it in a secure location so maybe someday it could benefit humanity.” lol not in current canon! And then Soong goes “Maybe instead of genetics, cybernetics are the future of humanity!” WE DIDN’T NEED THAT. BRENT SPINER BEING HERE IS THE NOD TO DATA BEING MADE. HIM BEING SOONG IS THE NOD TO THAT. WE KNOW WHAT HAPPENS!

The Forge - ENT 8/10

The crew plays Basketball. Turns out Phlox is fucking AMAZING as basketball, and he’s literally just standing there, catching the ball when his teammates send him the ball, and then shooting 3 pointers. Thats so funny. It’s interrupted by bad news: there’s been a bombing on Earth. The Vulcan embassy was blown up, and 12 Vulcans killed. 43 people over all. Admiral Forrest too, he saved the ambassador by covering him with his body. We did NOT make a good trade here. We traded the ONE GOOD ADMIRAL WE’VE EVER HAD for a shitty asshole.

Calling it now, the high command bombed themselves in order to justify some war with the Andorians or some nonsense. This would be the SECOND false flag operation tonight.

They believe it was either the Andorians or the Syrranites. The Syrranites are a religious cult on Vulcan who have always been peaceful but have recently become more zealous. They find a bomb, and find Vulcan DNA on it. Syrranites. Well, that’s a sign. The DNA is from a Vulcan named T’Pau… hey, T’Pau? That’s a name!

This is T’Pau, she appears in Amok Time in TOS. Fascinating.

Oh, the ambassador is actually really, REALLY unhappy about all of this. Archer is being shoved out of this investigation, and he goes to Archer and is like “Do NOT let them shove you out. You deserve more than this. Fuck them.” FINALLY nice to see him NOT be an asshole. And all it took was someone to die for him :)

T’Pol’s mom escapes into hiding, because she is apparently a Syrranite. T’Pol and Archer go down, sneaking onto Vulcan to find the truth. They are rescued from a Selat by a guy named Arev, who claims to be here to study Surak. Archer and T’Pol go with him.

Meanwhile, on Enterprise, they find the data implicating T’Pau is clearly false. It’s the DNA of her when she was a month old. But they need more info. They have someone who might have seen the actual cuprit, but they are in a coma. So the ambassador does the first ever mind meld with a human. It’s risky, and also mind melds are super illegal, but he’s willing to do what he needs. Using the mind meld, they find out, woah, it was actually the Vulcan High Command who did the bombing! What a FUCKING SURPRISE.

They confront the high command about this, despite knowing it’s A) illegal and B) they’re in on it. The Ambassador is hoping they not ALL in on it though. Turns out they’re in on it :)

Arev explains that the high command USED to just be about space exploration, but has taken over much more about society. So, this is actually interesting. I’ve made it NO secret I fucking hate how the Vulcans have been handled in this show. They’ve been extremely assholish for no reason, violent even. Hypocritical. I do not believe this was intended, but if this ends with “some fascists took over government because logic can be used to justify everything” and them being ousted, that’ll help.

Arev also explains Katras, and claims that his people are holding the Katra of Surak. Then, Arev is killed by a storm, but not before doing SOMETHING to Archer that makes him know some weird shit. They’re captured by Syrranites, TO BE CONTINUED.

Obviously Archer has the katra in him, but also like, why the fuck did they put the katra in HIM? And not the vulcan for whom this is culturally important? I mean he was there, I guess. But he’s just The Very Special Boy.

Awakening - ENT 7/10

The ambassador goes in front of the High Command. Turns out Stel, the dude who did the bombing, HAS been found guilty of it… because he’s a syrranite :D Wow what a surprise. The ambassador is NOT impressed, and is not buying it. This is a good scene.

And then we get a bad scene. Archer confronts T’Pau, and is very, VERY angry about it. He’s locked up, because of course he is.

Turns out yes, Archer was given Surak’s katra, and now Vulcan Jesus is up in his brain meats, and is like “Yo we’re stuck with each other. Time to put up with it.” The Syrranites find out about it, and are SURE he has Surak. T’Pol is like “This is dumb, there’s no such thing as Surak’s katra”. She’s really REALLY weirdly against everything the Syrranites speak for. I mean, I get it in way, it goes against everything she knew as high command person, but she’s been doubting them for THREE YEARS.

T’Pau knows a way to possibly remove the katra from Archer. It has a possibility of fucking killing him. Everyone is like “uh maybe don’t kill people I dunno how much Surak would like that” and she goes “I think I know what Surak would want :)” oh great, she’s awful too. Archer willingly goes through with it though, because alright. It’s gonna be forced upon him anyway.

Instead of Surak going to the random murderer who wants to take over the cult, he stays with Archer. What a surprise. He tells Archer that he’s our last hope, etc etc, Very Special Boy as I said.

Meanwhile, Enterprise has been trying to find the captain and tries to sneak a shuttle down. It doesn’t work, the High Command has kept an eye on them. It’s not either get the fuck away from Vulcan, or Earth is at war. Whoopsies!

“ATTACK OUR ALLIES” “Yes sir!” Oh good they’re Just Following Orders :) Though the rest of the council is like “whhhhat the fffuck are you doing???”

Archer is now like… Mystic Archer. And I guess I like that better than Angry Archer. But I’d still have prefered T’Pol.

Oh good we found The LEgendary Vulcan Tolberone. I bet that thing can hold a LOT of Eldrazi.

The High Command bombs the fuck out of the encampment after Enterprise gets out of scan range, which REALLY looks like massacring people after all the cameras are out of the way. Why the fuck does the high command let this one dude do this? T’Pol’s mom dies in the attack.

The ambassador tells Trip something bad. The high command is planning to do a pre-emptive strike on the Andorians, with faulty information about them creating weapons of mass destruction. Holy shit, it’s TOPICAL STUFF THAT’S NOT AWFUL???? Trip heads to Andoria to stop this.

Kir’Shara - Ent 7.5/10

We get a scene with the High Command. The lead asshole guy, V’Las, is really pushing for a pre-emptive strike against the Andorians. The others are like “We shouldn’t do that, all our data could be falsified” “Yes but what about THIS data?? shows more data showing the Andorians are doing things “oh okay” WHATEVER HAPPENED TO DATA CAN BE FALSIFIED? These fucking idiots, this is SO accurate about how fascists take over a government. Obvious open lies.

Also Reed is like “The Vulcans are in the right to attack the Andorians” holy SHIT WHY IS THIS GUY THE WORST??? Literally the worst Trek character.

SHRAN IS BACK! He’s their only real connection to Andoria, so it makes sense. They contact him, and tell him. He’s angry, but also believes them because A) he believes the pink skins and b) the ambassador is CLEARLY betraying his people. Why? For peace. Something Shran would prefer. Again, I love Shran. Violent, angry, xenophobic, always hoping for peace, whats good for his people. He’s great.

Hey, remember how T’Pol has a Psychic STD? T’Pau can fix it, because turns out, it’s actually an imbalance caused by unskilled melders. How do you fix it? Properly melding. I mean, if we’re following the HIV theming, she’s literally fucking the HIV out of her. Okay.

Archer is REALLY in on carrying this katra. He HAS to do this, he’s the Chosen One. Remember DS9, how for FIVE SEASONS Sisko resisted anything like this? Archer falls into it immediately. Okay dude.

Ohhh, Shran. He kidnaps Ambassador Koval and tortures him for the “true location of the fleet”. It’s basically the torture scene between Garak and Odo. Shran is NOT happy about doing this. But not nearly as good. I don’t dislike this in concept, but he should have tipped after a bit, but it goes on for a WHILE and he still doesn’t let up. He ONLY lets up when Enterprise shoots at him, and he either has to kill the Humans, who he respects, or give up. Only THEN does he give up. Come on, man.

Trip decides to side with the Andorians on their side during the battle. God damn. This is one of the most important things in the formation of the Federation, and it was NOT A THING ARCHER HAD ANYTHING TO DO WITH. It was DECIDED BY TRIP. THEY CAN’T EVEN STICK TO THEIR SPECIAL BOY.

T’Pau and Archer show up in the High Command with the Holicron and use it to tell everyone what is fucking OBVIOUS by this point, especially after V’Las had his guards almost kill his fellow council member. Why the hell did this guy have so much power? Anyways he’s shot and everything is okay.

Everything is getting better immediately! Koss divorces T’Pol, the Katra is easily removed from Archer, the High Command is dissolved, Earth stands on its own, and all is good. But then, plot twist…

THE ROMULANS WERE BEHIND IT! That’s a good setup if I’m being honest. The only thing I didn’t super like about this triple parter is how T’Pau was a weird piece of shit and then just never got her comeuppance or admitted she was wrong. Oh and Reed and Trip, but y’know, thats implied.

Daedalus - ENT 3/10

Enterprise is getting a visit from one Emory Erickson, the inventor of the transporter. He’s here to upgrade their transporter. He talks about all he’s had to go through as the inventor, including people being weird about the transporter. “There are even weirdos who ask if the person being made is not just a copy, and is even the same person and same soul!” lol ALL the trekkies I’ve ever met discuss this at one point.

We also learn that at some point, he lost a son, named Quinn. The weights of the past are weighing heavily on Emory. But Emory and his daughter are having a secret talk. They’re doing something sussy, though the daughgter is unhappy about doing this.

There’s an anomaly, and someone dies violently. The daughter is like “WOW fuck this, we TOTALLY killed someone, I’m out” but the dad going “No” stops her. Alright.

Trip is getting sussy vibes. He’s smart enough (so they say) to realize that Emory is dicking him around. They also capture images of the anomaly, and it’s obvious it’s Quinn. When Archer confronts him, Emory IMMEDIATELY folds and goes “yeah no I’m not here for anything but to bring Quinn back, sorry, but can you help me please?” WHY THE FUCK DIDN’T YOU JUST DO THIS TO BEGIN WITH, DUDE???

LMFAO Emory is like “You should hold back judgement on me until YOU’VE lost a son”, to Trip. The ONLY PERSON ON THIS CREW WHO HAS, IN FACT, LOST A SON LIVE ON THE SHOW.

Also, Emory says “oh my subspace transporter is a flawed concept and it will never work. It’s impossible to transport things at those distances, it’s not possible now and it won’t be possible in a thousand years.” YEAH, FUNNY FUCKING THING ABOUT THAT

Everyone is yelling in this fucking episode. Trip is like “maybe we shouldn’t do this before more people die, captain” and Archer screams at him about it. I do not like the idea of making Trip more likable by making Archer less likeable. Star Trek should not be a fucking Likability Slider.

The camera work in this episode is very strange, and that might be the first time I’ve mentioned camera work in a- wait no, that one shitty SNW episode with Beto doing all the camera work.

Quinn’s pattern had destabalized too much, and he’s irretrievable without dying. They force Emory to Let Him Go, and he dies in his dads arms. It’s not as emotional as they’d hope. Welp, glad someone died for this.

T’Pol basically breaks up with Trip, telling him she needs to focus on herself and her beliefs right now, which have been shaken, what with her entire government collapsing. He goes “well at least the warp drive still needs me”. Go fuck yourself, you asshat. Stick your dick in the warp engine.

entry.title: LizstarTrek - Enterprise Season 3 episodes 18-24
entry.content:
Every Sunday my Twitch subs and I watch Star Trek in my Discord. Here are my reviews and thoughts on each of the episodes as I see it. If you’re curious about a nerd’s views on Star Trek episodes, please read on, I go quite in depth with some of these episodes! I’ve watched some Star Trek before but not all of it!

IT’S BEEN A LOOOOONG ROOOOOAAAD

Azati Prime - ENT 4.5/10

Enterprise finally reaches Azati Prime, the planet they gaslit Degra to learn its location. Guess who’s already there? Degra! Dun dun dun! The other members of the Xindi Council are here, too. I love how one of them is just CLEARLY evil, the others are like “Yo good job, you’ve accomplished so much so soon!” “LET US NOT SPEAK OF ACCOMPLISHMENTS YET, NOT UNTIL THE HUMANS ARE EXTERMINATED.” Like, if I was on a political council and one of my coworkers talked like that, I’d do… SOMETHING about it, cause jesus.

Archer is about to sacrifice himself for a suicide run to blow up the weapon before he’s dragged to the future by Daniels. Daniels is like “look, this is all being run by the Sphere Builders who are using the Xindi to make the future better for them. If you sacrifice yourself, they’ll just build another weapon and nothing will change, AND we’ll never get the Federation, which btw, has Xindi in it. This beautiful future could be your’s if you just try diplomacy for once in your life.”

And what does Archer say?

“No.”

Straight up, Archer is the worst Star Trek captain? He often gets to the right spot in the end, but for some fucking reason he always has to start at the WORST FUCKING SPOT and then be FORCED there. He’s told “You can do diplomacy to stop this peacefully” and he goes “No, I want to kill people”. WHY IS THIS OUR HERO? Like, this is the culmination of the ENTIRE season. He later says he’s sacrificing himself cause he’s done a lot of horrible shit this season but MAYBE YOU SHOULDN’T HAVE DONE THAT, AND MAYBE THERE ARE BETTER WAYS TO MOVE FORWARD. If it’s trying to do a survivor guilt “I must get my penance” kinda story, it’s NOT telling it well, at all, because his violent aggressive war crimey bullshit he’s done the entire season has been the right choice in the story. He wouldn’t be here if he didn’t fucking kidnap and gaslight a scientist.

What a fucking surprise, Archer gets captured. What a wild twist. Fucking idiot.

On Enterprise, they’re suffering with possibly losing their captain cause he’s a fucking idiot. T’Pol is acting VERY weird.

After harassing some reptiles beating him up for a while, Archer decides to try the diplomacy route. Better late than never I guess. He asks for Degra, who shows up, and he makes it clear to Degra he knows a lot about him, saying “I know the name of your unborn dead third child”. “How do you know that?” “That’s not important right now” OH, ARCHER? NOT GONNA TELL HIM YOU KIDNAPPED HIM AND WIPED HIS MEMORY?? SEE, THIS IS WHAT I MEAN. That was a fucked up way to gather information, but WITHOUT IT, he would NOT be able to do this. Warcrimes are okay as long as you need to do them to succeed in stopping terrorism. Anyways he tells Degra about the Sphere Builders, the future, and that the Humans need to survive or the Xindi will die. He goes to his superiors, and while they don’t BELIEVE him, it puts some dents in their armor.

Anyways sudden jump cut, Enterprise is being blown up. It really was jarringly sudden. When Degra and the other non-Reptilian Xindi find out they’re like “But you said you’d hold off?” and he OBVIOUSLY EVIL REPTILIAN IS LIKE “I grew tired of waiting. And no, I won’t stop.” WHY ARE WE WORKING WITH THIS GUY? God, good thing there are no psychopathic manchildren in the real life government.

It’s actually funny because this Reptilian Xindi is a great example of what Republican politicians were like in 2003.

Meh.

Damage - ENT 1/10

Enterprise stops being blown up, only because they just decide not to kill them. The other Council Members have overruled the Reptilian one and are ordering him to give them Archer. He is NOT happy. The Aquatic are going to deliver Archer to the Council so they can talk to him.

Meanwhile, Enterprise is dangerously on fire, and T’Pol is still not doing well.

Ey, the Aquatic vessel is good. I dunno if we’ve EVER seen aquatic-based alien life in this series in this way? Anyways, he’s knocked out by gas, and then… is sent in an escape pod back to Enterprise. Weird. T’Pol is STILL acting weird. Her hands are shaking and she’s viewing the world warped and slowed. Ohhh NO, SHE HAS RADICAL SIX!!!

There’s a random shower sex scene with T’Pol and Trip in the middle of the episode and I hate it. But it’s just a dream. Hmm. I think Berman just wanted more horniness in his Trek again for his OC.

Some aliens show up, their ship hurt by this region of space. Archer is willing to help them, if they help too, because his ship is fuuuuucked. These are the Illyrians! They’re friendly and chill. Archer asks them for their warp coil, and they’re like “uh no, without that it’ll take us 3 years to get back home”. Okay, that’s off limits then :)

T’Pol risks her life to get some Trellium D, then crushes is up and injects it into herself. What in the fuck is this episode? Why is it like this? Why did they decide it was supposed to be like this? I thought for a second that her disease was getting out of hand, remember she does still have Vulcan AIDs, but no, it’s a drug metaphor. What the fuck?!

Archer has a conversation with Phlox. It’s well framed and dark, but like… Archer is like “I’m about to cross a line. One I never wanted to cross. But I have to, if the mission is to succeed.” OH, JUST NOW? YOU’VE BEEN DOING THIS ALL FUCKING SEASON, WHY STOP NOW?!

“In response to Archer saying (later in the scene) that a line he intended to cross would probably not be the last one, Phlox replied, “Sometimes you have to draw a line… sometimes you have to step over one… ethics are not the only consideration,” instead of merely responding, as he does in the final version of the scene, “Probably not.””

FUCKING CHRIST.

Archer makes his plan clear. He’s going to steal the Illyrians warp coil. What the fuck?

Back in the Council, we learn of their benefactor, the Female Changeli- I’m sorry, the Female Sphere Builder. She’s been guiding them and helping them, and admits to helping the Reptilians and Insectoids go back in the past to kill all Humans. But she was just doing it so that the council would stay stable, cause they were going to leave the council if they can’t do a genocide, she just wanted to help :) They CLEARLY do not trust her.

T’Pol confronts Archer about his plan. She even QUOTES THE QUOTE I OFTEN QUOTE TO MAKE FUN OF HIM. But it turns out, she’s only getting emotional and angry about it BECAUSE SHE’S A FUCKING DRUG ADDICT AND ISN’T IN HER RIGHT MIND, NOT BECAUSE IT’S ACTUALLY WRONG TO DO. After this, she relents and goes to the doctor to get help for her drug habits.

This is bullshit and terrible in a way that most bad Trek isn’t. It’s really, REALLY rough. Like, okay… this isn’t bad on paper? Some of this could be handled well. The crew having to do evil things but then realizing in the end that they can go the higher route or something, but we KNOW that’s not how this is going to end. And then the drug stuff? She could be out of her mind because of her Vulcan AIDs, or from residual Trellium poisoning, not because of the ACTUAL reason she gives, which is “I was finally able to access emotions and it became addicting”. THAT’S NOT HOW VULCANS WORK. Also it’s implied it’s because she wanted to fuck Trip more. That is SO UPSETTING TO ME I WANT TO FUCKING VOMIT.

This is just reminding me of how they fucked up the Equinox storyline. They realize shit is evil, Archer is admitting he’s doing evil, and it’s not framed heroically. But it doesn’t actually handle it well, because of the way the characters handle it. This is not going to dwell on them, their hypocrisy doesn’t effect them, they will get over it next week. When Sisko did this, he had to repeat to himself over and over “I can get over this” BECAUSE HE COULDN’T. AND THEN HE NEVER DID ANYTHING LIKE IT AGAIN, BECAUSE IT WAS A ONE TIME I HAVE TO DO THIS THING. ARCHER’S BEEN DOING THIS ALL FUCKING SEASON WITH NO PROBLEMS.

Anyways they steal the fucking warp coil and strand the Illyrians 3 years from home in a region of space with dangerous anomalies that kills 90% of the people who enter it.

This fucking sucked.

This episode sucked SO BAD that we decided to watch movies instead. See you next week for the end of Season 3. Thank god.

Okay its next week. Let’s get this over with.

The Forgotten - ENT 3/10

18 people died over the nonsense that happened in the last two episodes. The episode REALLY hammers this in. You know, inbetween THIS.

T’Pol being a drug addict is not a thing that’ll that’ll be ending any time soon. She’s just… emotional now. Well, she wasn’t really acting like a Vulcan anyway.

Enterprise meets up with Degra and the Arboreal council member who’s backing Archer. They arranged for Archer to be returned to his ship, and want him to prove that he can time travel. That’s gonna be… hard, but he offers to show them proof if they come aboard his ship.

You know, I was told something about this season before I watched it. They described it as “the most submarine coded season of Star Trek”. I was looking forward to that. A crummy ship in a dangerous area where they can only kinda pop out occasionally and gotta patch up holes. But now that we’re here, uh, it sucks. They use it to justify straight up killing hte Illyrians, and Tucker’s reaction to this is to not sleep for 2 days as he gets crabbier and crabbier. I’m not sure why I was ever looking forward to that to begin with.

Seth Macfarlane has a cameo in this. He says two sentences to Trip and looks about as excited to talk to him as I would be.

Trip meets with Degra and yells at him about how his weapon blew up Florida. And also Cuba, Venezuala, prolly some of Jamaica… But those don’t matter. Only Florida matters, cause that’s ‘Murica. This’d be like if 9/11 happened and also a bunch of other countries got attacked, but everything was treated exactly the same. I fucking HATE how America-centric this show is.

There’s a plasma leak, and an eepy Tucker needs to go out with Reed to fix it. God, imagine if we had ANY ENGINEERS THAT AREN’T TRIP who could help him?! Well we did, one of them died, we had never seen her before, and Trip has been spending the episode complaining about needing to write to her parents.

Despite all of Trip’s being a shithead, and all of the lack of evidence Archer is giving (just show him the fucking future shit that Daniels left), Degra is throwing his hat in with the Humans, and wants to help them. Though when a Reptilian warship shows up to finish the job extrajudicially, he’s not exactly ready or willing to join the Humans and attack the Reptilians… at first, but just a LITTLE convincing and he joins them to fight. Good thing Degra’s one of the good one- oh, he went WAY too far and just straight up KILLED those Reptilians. Welp, whatever, they would have hurt our alliance, so it’s GOOD that they died!

Degra convinces Archer go to in front of the council and show his evidence (what fucking evidence?!), but it’ll take three days via a subspace corridor, which some kind of beings use as hunting grounds. I’m sure that won’t be a problem.

Trip finally writes that letter to the dead crewman’s parents. He makes it about himself and his dead sister. I am going to become the joker.

E² - ENT 6/10

The episode starts with Old T’Pol with Land of Confusion-style makeup talking to some Vulcan like “we could not stop the 7 million people from dying, so you must find Jonathan Archer”. Interesting.

As they approach the corridor, they are approached by another Starfleet ship. It’s Enterprise, looking EXTREMELY shitty and awful. They tell Archer to hold back and not enter the nebula… they come over and the Vulcan guy introduces himself as Lorian. He tells a story, which is shown in like 5FPS shitty cam footage. Why?? I dunno. Anyways when Enterprise goes through the corridor, it’ll send them back 120 years. In the past, they have a plan, to stop the first Xindi attack. They obviously failed, and now they want to help team up to stop the second.

This is YET ANOTHER case of this show just taking a previous episode I like, and almost certainly doing it worse. This is just the DS9 episode where they go down to a planet with time shit, and find their descendants. The writer actually had an original plan of it being the Columbia, Enterprise’s sister ship, who got sent back instead, but was overruled by executives who were like “eh, it’s been long enough since DS9 aired”. I don’t like THAT, but I don’t mind it that much so far. And I love this kind of “AU who pares off with who” storyline.

You know the Hot Lady Marine that we see every now and then? Her name is Amanda, and Phlox apparently shacks up with her. Phlox can GET IT. Phlox won Enterprise, hell yeah.

Aaaand the twist, The Future Enteprise is actually evil. Lorian makes plans to steal some Enterprise tech so that they can go and meet the Xindi and stop them. I was thinking they’d do this so that they can justify their own existence and cause themselves to exist, like the DS9 episode, but no, they’re just kinda doing it because they think this is the best chance of stopping the Xindi. That’s a LOT less interesting.

Also, it’s bad and evil that they’re stealing engineering hardware. Piracy is bad again!

Lorian is stopped. His crew won’t let him just KILL the others when he wants to. In jail, he explains why he did what he “had to do”. Because he’s guilty he couldn’t stop the weapon. Dog, SO MANY people could have stopped this weapon, there’s time travel in this storyline.

Old T’Pol tells Young T’Pol the best way to handle her emotions. Use Trip as a fucktoy to get all the emotions out. I am only BARELY exaggerating.

Shout outs to Twinborne in my chat for this nonsense.

The Enterprises work together, and they basically tow the Current Enterprise out of the corridor, while sacrificing themselves. The Future Enterprise’s fate is not shown, but the Current Enterprise made it through, into the right century. That problem solved itself I guess.

Enteprise meets with Degra, the end. At least this episode shows Phlox has the most game on Enterprise.

The Council - ENT 7.5/10

The episode starts with a council of Sphere Builders yelling at each other about KILLING ALL HUMANS, floating in a white void. Damn bitch, y’all live like this? No wonder you wanna enter the Beta Quadrant.

Hoshi and Archer go to the council. Holy SHIT, Hoshi gets to DO SOMETHING? MEanwhile, T’Pol, Mayweather, Reed, and a Redshirt are doing…. SOMETHING at a sphere. Degra and Archer are nearly stopped by Dolim, the Reptilian councilmember, but they make through after all.

REALLY good Archer, amazing diplomacy skills. You start your talking before the council to make your case that they’re being lied to by going “You killed 7 million Humans, I fucking hate you”. Very good.

VERY American worldview, frankly.

It doesn’t go well. What a fucking surprise. The Reptilians and the Insectoids are NEVER going to side with the Humans, but maybe the Aquatics will. They need to focus on convincing them, and they respond best to hard visual evidence. Meanwhile, the Sphere Builder woman, or “The Guardian” as they call her, is behind the Reptilian telling him to make sure the weapon launches so that the Reptilians rule over a unified Xindi empire. Yeah, an empire of One species.

Meanwhile, the B team are in a Sphere, prolly intending to get something that proves their case. The Red Shirt is waving his death flags all over the fuckin’ place. Oh shit he died, no way.

After showing the dead Sphere Builder, the council is a liiiittle more reticent to learning more. And then, they get their three votes to postpone the launching of the weapon… Not the Aquatics! The Reptilians! Glad they’re finally coming around :D Dolim comes around to have a peaceful meeting with Degra- oh, nope.

RIP Degra.

Obviously this pissed off EVERYONE on the council, except the Insectoids, who are ontologically evil. I do kinda wish it was JUST one group, the Reptilian leader and his yesmen, pushing for this after all the evidence. The Insectoids and Reptilians leave the council, launch the weapon, and they can’t stop it as it leaves to Earth. But not before stealing THE FEMALE (Hoshi). Oh no! Time for I AM LOCUTUS OF THE REPTILIANS, RESISTANCE IS- no, prolly not.

Pacing is a little off but pretty alright actually.

Countdown - ENT 6/10

We start with Hoshi being tied to a chair and tortured. They intend to use her to hack the code so they can activate The Weapon. Poor fucking Hoshi. FINALLY taken out of mothballs just to be tortured.

Everyone on Enterprise is like “I sure with Hoshi was here to help us with this thing”. Y’all made do without her this entire fucking season, why is it a problem now?

They need the Aquatics help to go after The Weapon, but the Aquatics are fine with being neutral in this new civil war. Archer tries to diplomacy them to their side, but is VERY bad at diplomacy, so instead he says they know how to disable the Spheres, and will teach them if they help him. They cannot do that. So now Trip and T’Pol need to find out how to shut down the Spheres. Whoops.

The Aquatics agree. Now, after eight months in the Expanse, it’s time to leave and stop that weapon. The battle begins. The aquatics smuggle Enterprise in a giant carrier, which is kinda neat, and then there’s a big battle. Even the MACO get their chance to FINALLY do SOMETHING. They board a Xindi vessel to try and save Hoshi and stop the weapon from launching. This looking VERY good for our heroes.

The Sphere Builders use the spheres to create MASSIVE anomalies that destroy a lot of the attacking fleet. Shit Gets Bad real fast. The giant Aquatic ship falls apart, kinda sad. And then the MACO struggle to survive against the Reptilians, and the leader is killed in action, after everyone else is teleported out. They can’t stop them. The weapon is launched.

Also the Reptilians betray the Insectoids, who are like “Yo actually that anomaly shows the Guardians DID build the spheres”. Oh no, the leopards!

Plans are made to chase it and send a team inside to stop it. Hoshi is EXTREMELY injured, and moving her might kill her, but Archer demands she be moved anyway. Come on, man. Also, the rest of Enterprise head to a Sphere to blow it up, which should blow up the entirety of them.

Eh, it’s okay.

Zero Hour - ENT 6/10

This is the FINAL episode where Brennan and Braga will be writing…. until the VERY end of the series. We’re almost free of them. But their shadow remains.

We have to watch the Reptilian eat some mice. Everyone hated that. I guess they’re Snake People now.

Wow, what a surprise, Archer is REALLY handling the Hoshi thing wrong. She’s not sure where she is, because she has BRAIN DAMAGE, and he’s yelling at her and shaking her to JUST STOP BEING HYSTERICAL, WOMAN. Bitches just need a bit of Percusive Maintenence. He also intends to TAKE HER BACK ONTO THE WEAPON. Dude.

Daniels shows up one more time to tell Archer he’s The Very Special Boy who needs to live so the Federation can happen, so just sacrifice one of the others, Reed or Hoshi, they aren’t important. The more this guy shows up the more I hate him.

HE’S HERE! The Andorians are here to help hold the Reptilians off so that Archer can sneak on board. God, I love Shran. Thanks for being the only good part of Enterprise, Shran. “AND TELL ARCHER WE’RE NOT EVEN ANYMORE. HE OWES ME.” God, you’re great.

We have two tense moments here. Archer is hacking into the weapon from the inside, using Hoshi’s plans, while Enterprise is sitting in intense radiation and trying to blow up a sphere while the Builders try to stop them. Shit just keeps getting worse and worse. Hoshi loses her notes, people continue to die.

Christ, the special effects are horrifying.
